sentences.base_language: Good morning! Are you ready for the meeting?
sentences.target_language: صباح الخير! هل أنت مستعد للاجتماع؟
sentences.romanized: sabah al-khayr! hal anta musta'idd lil-ijtima'?

sentences.base_language: Yes, I prepared the report yesterday.
The meeting starts at nine o'clock.
The manager wants to discuss the new project.
How many people are on your team?
We have five engineers and two designers.
I send many emails every day.
sentences.target_language: أرسل رسائل بريد إلكتروني كثيرة كل يوم.
sentences.romanized: ursil rasa'il barid iliktruní kathira kull yawm.
sentences.literal: I-send messages mail electronic many every day.

sentences.base_language: My colleague helps me with difficult tasks.
I finish work at five in the afternoon.
After work, I like to relax at home.
sentences.target_language: بعد العمل، أحب الاسترخاء في البيت.
sentences.romanized: ba'd al-amal, uhibb al-istirkha' fi al-bayt.
sentences.grammarNote: بعد (ba'd) = after. In Arabic, بعد + definite noun is common: بعد العمل = after work, بعد الاجتماع = after the meeting.

sentences.base_language: My job is challenging but rewarding.
